_comment: Macro-Terrain: Vermilion Bird Ember Mountains. Per scale directive: this terrain IS a being. Player shouldn't initially realize they're standing on bone. Divine Sense -> Perception changes -> 'You realize this mountain range... is one finger.' Prime Directive: the being's corpse is objectively terrain; perception tier determines what the player sees.
name: Vermilion Bird Ember Mountains
scale: 300 li long — the shed feathers of a divine bird.
canonical_event: The Vermilion Bird nested here before ascending to the Divine Sect.

perception_tiered_appearance.mortal: A mountain range of red stone, perpetually warm, peaks glow at dusk.
perception_tiered_appearance.qi_condensation: The mountains radiate fire-essence; the stone is warm year-round.
perception_tiered_appearance.foundation_establishment: The mountains are petrified feathers; the glow is residual divine fire.
perception_tiered_appearance.core_formation: This is where the Vermilion Bird once nested; the mountains are its shed feathers.
perception_tiered_appearance.nascent_soul: The divine fire still smolders; cultivating here grants fire-essence.
perception_tiered_appearance.spirit_severing: The Vermilion Bird's nest-essence permeates the range; the divine bird returns here.

perception_mechanic.trigger: When player's perception tier rises, the appearance re-rolls automatically.
perception_mechanic.no_locked_upgrades: A mortal who later reaches Core Formation will see the truth; the terrain does not change, only understanding does.
perception_mechanic.canon_fidelity: This is exactly how Wang Lin perceived Ancient God Tu Si's remains — first as terrain, then as god-bone, then as god-memory.
